thesis: World's third-largest gold producer with Tier-1 jurisdictional exposure, record margins, and a fortress balance sheet. Q1 2026 net income of $1.7B and FCF of $730M demonstrate exceptional gold price leverage. Stock has corrected 36% from highs, improving risk/reward for long-term accumulators.

drivers: Gold sustains $5,500–$6,000/oz on continued central bank buying and geopolitical tension
Detour Lake expansion adds 100k+ oz/year by 2027–2028
Fosterville turnaround reduces group AISC below $1,400/oz
AEM uses balance sheet strength for accretive M&A

risks: Gold drops below $4,000/oz on Fed hawkishness / real yield surge
AISC inflation exceeds guidance, compressing margins
Operational disruption at Detour or Canadian Malartic
USD strength raises costs at non-North American assets

base_case.assumptions: Gold averages $4,500–$5,000/oz in 2026–2027. Production meets 3.3–3.5M oz guidance. AISC stays within $1,400–$1,550/oz range. Dividend grows 8–12% annually.

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 Earnings — production and cost update
Gold price movement / Federal Reserve policy shifts

key_risks: Gold price volatility — pure-play leverage cuts both ways
Cost inflation in energy, labor, and consumables
FX exposure (EUR, AUD, MXN) vs. USD
Permitting or operational delays at core assets
